exemplar.title: How do (variations) in muscle fibre composition of the quadriceps influence the completion time of the Bronco Fitness Test in semi-professional male rugby players aged 16-18?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

detailedExemplarData.title: How do (variations) in muscle fibre composition of the quadriceps influence the completion time of the Bronco Fitness Test in semi-professional male rugby players aged 16-18?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

strengths: Clear, personal link between rugby experience and research question
Distinct evidence of independent thinking in experimental design and reflection
Consistent personal initiative in developing consent processes and control measures
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments uniformly placed this work in the top band for Personal Engagement. The student demonstrates genuine curiosity, original design choices, and thorough reflective commentary throughout. No significant gaps were identified, justifying the full mark of 2.

strengths: Highly relevant and focused research question with clear rationale
Comprehensive background drawing on primary studies
Robust safety and ethical protocols (consent, PAR-Q, anonymisation)
weaknesses: Proxy measurement (vertical-jump test) remains somewhat ambiguous
Methodology has minor limitations (single Bronco trial, lack of instrument calibration discussion)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall the Exploration is strongâbackground and ethics are entirely appropriate and relevant, and the research question is focused. Methodological choices are well justified, with only minor limitations. These nearâtop-band qualities merit a score in the highest band, adjusted to 5 to reflect the few remaining weaknesses.

strengths: Large sample size with sufficient raw data for robust conclusions
Accurate processing of means, standard deviations, CV and ANOVA
Insightful interpretation linking statistics to the hypothesis and noting outliers
weaknesses: Uncertainty discussion does not propagate errors into processed statistics
Raw vertical-jump values omitted, limiting independent verification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis shows mostly top-band performance: precise statistical work and valid interpretation. Some discussion of measurement uncertainty is present, but propagation into final values is absent. These factors justify a high score of 5 within the 0â6 range.

strengths: Well-supported and relevant conclusions tied to statistical findings
Clear understanding of methodological limitations and their impact
weaknesses: Comparisons to broader scientific context are brief
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The Evaluation excels in identifying and correcting methodological issues and proposing improvements. Conclusions are relevant though not fully integrated into the wider literature. This mix of strong and moderate strands supports a robust score of 5.

strengths: Clear overall presentation with coherent headings, figures and tables
Consistent use of subject-specific terminology with minimal errors
weaknesses: Formatting glitches (e.g., missing axis labels, inconsistent bibliography style)
Occasional verbosity and minor typographical errors
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication is consistently clear and well structured, with effective use of terminology. Minor formatting and editorial issues prevent perfection but fall within the top performance band, justifying the maximum score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: Stop-watch Â±0.5 s and human reaction Â±0.5 s are discussed and combined; error bars show SD. There is no treatment of uncertainty in processed data or vertical-jump measurement. Thus âsome considerationâ (band 3-4) with a mid score of 3.
The student references Jardine et al. and Hopwood et al., offering limited critical comparison. Connections are correct but brief, fitting âsome relevant comparisonâ (score 3).

IA follows conventional structure (PE, background, method, data, analysis, evaluation, conclusion) and information is generally in logical order. Some duplication and appendix placement issues prevent the upper score; hence 3.
For each limitation, the student offers concrete, realistic improvements (reschedule turf, multiple trials, incentives). Suggestions are detailed and relevant, justifying a score of 5 in top band.

The research question is specific (muscle-fibre composition of quadriceps vs Bronco time in 16-18-year-old male rugby players) and aligns directly with measured variables, though minor ambiguity remains over how fibre type is determined, hence ârelevant but not fully focusedâ earns the top of the 3-4 band.

Limitation table identifies methodological weaknesses (biopsy vs jump test, slippery turf, motivation, timing) and explains their impact, showing clear methodological insight. Minor omissions (e.g., sampling bias) prevent perfect 6, so score 5.

The procedure controls many variables and includes large sample size, but the key measurement (vertical jump â fibre type) is weak and only one Bronco trial is taken. Method therefore âmainly appropriate with limitationsâ, meriting the lower half of band 3-4.

The background section cites several primary studies, explains fibre physiology and test demands, and considers confounding factors. Coverage is largely appropriate, yet lacks depth on validity of vertical-jump as a fibre proxy, so âmainly appropriate and relevantâ (band 3-4, upper end).
Narrative remains on-task; while wordy, irrelevant digressions are rare. This sustains reader focus, fitting score 3 in the highest band.

Interpretation links statistics to hypothesis, comments on outliers and overlaps, and recognises confounders. Occasional over-statement (e.g., calling results âunequivocallyâ reliable) limits full detail; hence upper band 3-4 with score 4.

Report is largely readable with headings, figures and tables, though some formatting glitches and long paragraphs reduce polish. This matches âclear presentation with minimal errorsâ, lower score in 3-4 band.

The student clearly links the investigation to personal rugby experience, formulates an original research idea and designs a bespoke two-part procedure. Reflections on preliminary trials, measurement choices and control of cheating all illustrate noticeable independent thinking and ownership of the work, fulfilling the descriptor for clear engagement.

Means, SD, CV and one-way ANOVA are calculated correctly and presented; uncertainties are not propagated, but numerical work is generally accurate. This satisfies âappropriate and accurateâ, score 5 (lower half of top band).

The justification explicitly states why the question matters to the student (frequent Bronco testing, curiosity about factors influencing performance). The narrative shows genuine curiosity and personal significance rather than a generic rationale, matching the top-band descriptor.

Raw data include 34 individual results, covering all fibre categories, sufficient to underpin statistical testing. Minor omission of vertical-jump raw heights prevents full marks, so a 5 within band 5-6 is appropriate.

Methodology, control table, consent process and limitation discussions were developed and executed by the student, demonstrating clear personal initiative throughout planning, implementation and reflection.

Consent forms, anonymity, PAR-Q screening, therapist on call and discussion of injury risk indicate clear awareness, though not every risk (COVID, crowding) is addressed. This fits âsome awarenessâ at the upper end of band 3-4.
Appropriate SEHS terminology (fast-twitch, CV, ANOVA) is used correctly and citations provided. A few typographical / referencing errors occur, so minimal rather than no errorsâscore 3.

Conclusion restates key findings and is supported by averages and P-value, but some sweeping claims about causation remain. This aligns with ârelevant conclusion with some supportâ, top of band 3-4.

annotations.comment: Background physiology is described well. To strengthen Exploration, the student should deepen the critique of vertical jump height as a proxy for fibre type, citing validation studies.

The studentâs narrative in the Personal Engagement section effectively links the investigation to personal rugby experience, demonstrating clear ownership and independent thinking throughout design and reflection.
The research question is clearly stated but remains ambiguous regarding how muscle fibre composition will actually be measured, limiting focus. Consider specifying the proxy measurement validity.
Material list omits discussion of measurement reliability (e.g., tapeâmeasure calibration). Including instrument precision would improve methodological rigor.
A single preliminary trial limits confidence in the VJT protocol. Consider conducting multiple pilot jumps to refine instructions and reduce measurement error.
Only one Bronco trial is planned, which severely limits reliability. Could the student schedule additional trials or consider a less demanding proxy for repeated measures?
The description of the independent variable suggests manipulation; in fact fibre type is measured. The student should reword to clarify that fibre type is an observational grouping.
Uncertainty discussion omits propagation into processed statistics. To strengthen Analysis, propagate timing and jump measurement error through mean calculations.
The control-variable table is comprehensive. Consider adding ambient conditions (temperature/humidity) to control for performance effects on sprint runs.
Interviewing athletes immediately post-test yields minimal data. To better explore confounders, consider a structured questionnaire administered after appropriate rest.
Safety and ethics are well addressed with consent, PAR-Q, anonymisation and firstâaid plans, showing clear awareness of participant welfare.
Observations are described but not integrated into data analysis or used to adjust results. To improve Analysis, discuss how wet turf or motivation affected specific data points.
The admission of imprecise jump measurements is honest. Future work could use a jump mat or video analysis to improve precision and consistency.
The large sample of 34 participants is a key strength, providing sufficient raw data for statistical testing and supporting more robust conclusions.
Raw data table omits recording of vertical jump heights for each subject; include these to allow verification of fibreâtype classification thresholds.
Outlier in Table 3 is highlighted effectively. For consistency, apply the same rule across all tables and state the exact IQR criteria used.
Processed data table correctly shows mean, SD and CV. This accurate data processing supports clear interpretation in subsequent analysis.
Figure 5âs axes lack unit labels and scale markers. Clear axis titles and tick marks are needed to enhance Communication and readability.
Use of a one-way ANOVA with reporting of P-value is appropriate and demonstrates accurate statistical processing.
The choice of an ANOVA Single Factor test is justified, but the student should discuss assumption checks (e.g., normality, equal variance) to fully validate the test choice.
Data interpretation links statistical results to the hypothesis with clarity and detail, aiding reader understanding of relevance and significance.
The explanation of bar-chart choice conflates qualitative/quantitative variables. Muscle fibre type is categorical; clarify this choice and its implications.
Some sentences in the interpretation are incomplete, interrupting flow. Ensure full continuity and avoid dangling fragments in final write-up.
The student notes contradictory implications of overlapping error bars vs ANOVA outcomes. A deeper critical discussion reconciling these would strengthen Evaluation.
The limitations table is exceptionally detailed, with realistic and relevant suggestions for improvement across methodological, practical and ethical domains.
The discussion of overlapping error bars in text is verbose and could be more conciseâfocus on key statistical implications rather than lengthy hypothetical scenarios.
Reflective commentary on managing logistics and team cooperation shows strong personal engagement and initiative, adding depth to the reportâs strengths.
The conclusion references one comparative study but lacks critical assessment of broader scientific context. Include more studies for a more robust justification.
For future studies, specify measurable outcomes and detail alternative fibreâtyping methods (e.g., ultrasound, NIRS) to improve the design of subsequent investigations.
Bibliography entries show inconsistent formatting (missing DOIs, varied styles). Apply a consistent citation style (e.g., APA) to improve Communication.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: Justification shows little personal significance or curiosity
Little evidence of personal input in design, implementation or presentation
Limited evidence of personal engagement with little independent thinking
Clear evidence of personal input and initiative throughout the investigation
Clear evidence of personal engagement with significant independent thinking
Justification demonstrates clear personal significance and curiosity

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Evidence of personal engagement and independent thinking

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: Superficial or limited relevance background information
Research question stated but not focused
Methodology appropriate to very limited extent
Mainly appropriate and relevant background information
Relevant but not fully focused research question
Entirely appropriate and relevant background information
Highly appropriate methodology addressing all significant factors
Relevant and fully focused research question
Sufficient relevant raw data supporting detailed conclusion
Basic outline of practical issues only
Some relevant comparison to scientific context
Detailed, relevant and fully supported conclusion
Clear understanding of methodological issues demonstrated
Correct and justified comparison to scientific context
Many errors in terminology and conventions
Appropriate use of terminology with minimal errors
Well structured with all necessary information
